title: agropyron cristatum subsp. desertorum
intro: Agropyron cristatum subsp. desertorum, also known as spike needlegrass, is a perennial grass species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Agropyron cristatum subsp. desertorum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the dried leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
